key_insight: A unified analytical model can diagnose where photoelectrochemical cells lose energy and translate those losses into concrete design improvements across different device architectures.
engineering_relevance: The framework gives engineers a quantitative way to compare materials, fit experimental I-V curves, and prioritize interventions such as nanostructuring or co-catalyst integration.
method: Unified loss-analysis model for both BIJ and SEJ photoelectrodes
Generates I-V curves, efficiency metrics, and efficiency maps under ideal/real conditions
Fits experimental data to decompose losses into thermodynamic, optical, recombination, and interfacial terms
evidence_signal: The abstract says the framework was validated against literature results spanning solar water splitting, CO2 reduction, NH3 synthesis, and solar redox flow batteries, including comparisons across Si, perovskites, hematite, and BiVO4.
watch_item: Performance diagnosis depends on fitting available current-voltage data, so accuracy may be limited by data quality, model assumptions, and how well a real device matches the idealized parameterization.
paper_title: From Loss Diagnosis to Rational Design: A Unified Analytical Model for Photoelectrochemical Cells
